description_long: This object consists of four rectangular stone blocks arranged in a staggered, stacked formation. The blocks have a smooth, slightly rounded finish with visible wood-like grain patterns, giving them a natural and uniform appearance. Each block is of similar size, and the arrangement creates a stepped effect, with one block set diagonally atop the others. The coloration is a light tan, resembling beech or maple wood, though the object is intended to represent stone. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering on the surfaces. The overall structure suggests use as a modular barrier or decorative obstacle in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A set of four light tan, rectangular stone blocks with smooth, rounded edges, stacked in a staggered, stepped arrangement. The blocks have a uniform, wood-like grain pattern and no visible markings.
description_view_0: Front view shows two vertically aligned blocks with smooth, rounded edges and a uniform tan color.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the staggered, stepped arrangement of all four blocks, including one block set diagonally atop the others.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the vertical alignment of two blocks, similar to the front, with visible grain texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the diagonal placement of the top block and the overall stepped structure.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or landscaped park barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained with no chips or discoloration.
